question: Is zero a rational number? Can you write it in the form p/q, where p and q are integers and q ‚Č† 0?
subject: mathematics
answer: Yes. 0 = 0/1 = 0/2 = 0/(-3) etc., all in p/q form with q ‚Č† 0.

question: Find three rational numbers between 3/5 and 4/5.
subject: mathematics
answer: Multiply numerator and denominator by 4: 12/20 and 16/20. Three rationals between them: 13/20, 14/20 (= 7/10), 15/20 (= 3/4).

question: Rationalise the denominator: 1 / (‚ąö7 ‚ąí ‚ąö6).
subject: mathematics
answer: Multiply by (‚ąö7 + ‚ąö6)/(‚ąö7 + ‚ąö6): = (‚ąö7 + ‚ąö6) / (7 ‚ąí 6) = ‚ąö7 + ‚ąö6.

question: Simplify: (‚ąö5 + ‚ąö2)¬≤ + (‚ąö5 ‚ąí ‚ąö2)¬≤.
subject: mathematics

question: If p(x) = x¬≤ ‚ąí 4x + 3, find the value of p(0) + p(1) + p(2).
subject: mathematics

question: Factorise: x¬≥ ‚ąí 23x¬≤ + 142x ‚ąí 120.
subject: mathematics
answer: p(1) = 1 ‚ąí 23 + 142 ‚ąí 120 = 0, so (x ‚ąí 1) is a factor. Dividing: x¬≥ ‚ąí 23x¬≤ + 142x ‚ąí 120 = (x ‚ąí 1)(x¬≤ ‚ąí 22x + 120) = (x ‚ąí 1)(x ‚ąí 10)(x ‚ąí 12).

question: Write the coordinates of the point which lies on the x-axis and is at a distance of 4 units to the right of the y-axis.
subject: mathematics

question: Plot the points A(2, 3), B(‚ąí2, 3), C(‚ąí2, ‚ąí3), D(2, ‚ąí3) on the Cartesian plane and name the figure ABCD.
subject: mathematics
answer: The four points form a rectangle (AB = CD = 4 units, BC = AD = 6 units, all angles 90¬į).

question: In a triangle ABC, if ‚ą†A = 55¬į and ‚ą†B = 65¬į, find ‚ą†C. Also, identify the longest side.
subject: mathematics
answer: ‚ą†C = 180¬į ‚ąí 55¬į ‚ąí 65¬į = 60¬į. The largest angle is ‚ą†B = 65¬į, so the longest side is AC (side opposite ‚ą†B).

question: The angles of a triangle are in the ratio 3 : 5 : 7. Find all three angles.
subject: mathematics
answer: Let angles = 3x, 5x, 7x. Then 15x = 180¬į, x = 12¬į. Angles are 36¬į, 60¬į, 84¬į.

question: A triangular park has sides 120 m, 80 m and 50 m. A gardener has to put a fence all around it and also plant grass inside. How much area does he need to plant? (Use Heron's formula.)
subject: mathematics
topic: area of triangle using Heron's formula

question: The mean of 5 observations is 4.4. If three of the observations are 1, 2 and 6, and the other two are x and y with x ‚ąí y = 3, find x and y.
subject: mathematics
answer: Sum = 4.4 √ó 5 = 22. So x + y = 22 ‚ąí 1 ‚ąí 2 ‚ąí 6 = 13. With x ‚ąí y = 3: x = 8, y = 5.

question: A coin is tossed 500 times and tail appears 275 times. What is the probability of getting a head?
subject: mathematics

question: Find the curved surface area and total surface area of a cylinder whose radius is 7 cm and height is 20 cm. (Use ŌÄ = 22/7.)
subject: mathematics

question: In a parallelogram ABCD, ‚ą†A = 70¬į. Find ‚ą†B, ‚ą†C and ‚ą†D.
subject: mathematics
answer: In a parallelogram, adjacent angles are supplementary. ‚ą†B = 180¬į ‚ąí 70¬į = 110¬į, ‚ą†C = ‚ą†A = 70¬į, ‚ą†D = ‚ą†B = 110¬į.

question: If x + y = 12 and xy = 27, find the value of x¬≥ + y¬≥.
subject: mathematics

question: Verify: x¬≥ + y¬≥ + z¬≥ ‚ąí 3xyz = (1/2)(x + y + z)[(x ‚ąí y)¬≤ + (y ‚ąí z)¬≤ + (z ‚ąí x)¬≤].
subject: mathematics
answer: Expand RHS: (1/2)(x+y+z)[x¬≤‚ąí2xy+y¬≤ + y¬≤‚ąí2yz+z¬≤ + z¬≤‚ąí2zx+x¬≤] = (1/2)(x+y+z)(2x¬≤+2y¬≤+2z¬≤‚ąí2xy‚ąí2yz‚ąí2zx) = (x+y+z)(x¬≤+y¬≤+z¬≤‚ąíxy‚ąíyz‚ąízx) = x¬≥+y¬≥+z¬≥‚ąí3xyz = LHS. Verified.

question: Which of the following is not a polynomial? (A) ‚ąö3 x¬≤ ‚ąí 2‚ąö3 x + 5  (B) x + 1/x  (C) 5‚ąöx ‚ąí 4  (D) x¬≤ ‚ąí 3x + 7
subject: mathematics
answer: (C) 5‚ąöx ‚ąí 4, because ‚ąöx = x^(1/2) has a fractional exponent, so it is not a polynomial.

question: If x + y = 12 and xy = 32, find the value of x¬≤ + y¬≤.
subject: mathematics

question: Express 0.6ŐĄ (0.6 repeating) in the form p/q.
subject: mathematics

question: Simplify: (8 + ‚ąö5)(8 ‚ąí ‚ąö5).
subject: mathematics

question: In which quadrant or on which axis do each of the points (‚ąí2, 4), (3, ‚ąí1), (‚ąí1, 0), (1, 2) and (‚ąí3, ‚ąí5) lie?
subject: mathematics
answer: (‚ąí2, 4): II quadrant; (3, ‚ąí1): IV quadrant; (‚ąí1, 0): negative x-axis; (1, 2): I quadrant; (‚ąí3, ‚ąí5): III quadrant.

question: Write the equation 2x + 3y = 6 in the form ax + by + c = 0 and indicate the values of a, b and c.
subject: mathematics

question: Draw the graph of 2x + y = 6 and find the coordinates where it meets the axes.
subject: mathematics
answer: At x = 0: y = 6, point (0, 6). At y = 0: x = 3, point (3, 0). Plot and join these two points to get the line.

question: In the figure, if AB ‚ą• CD, EF ‚ä• CD and ‚ą†GED = 126¬į, find ‚ą†AGE, ‚ą†GEF and ‚ą†FGE.
subject: mathematics
answer: ‚ą†GEF = ‚ą†GED ‚ąí 90¬į = 36¬į. Since AB ‚ą• CD, ‚ą†AGE = ‚ą†GED = 126¬į (alternate interior). ‚ą†FGE = 180¬į ‚ąí ‚ą†AGE = 54¬į (angles on a straight line with EF ‚ä• CD means adjustments apply; ‚ą†FGE = 180¬į ‚ąí 126¬į = 54¬į).

question: ABCD is a quadrilateral in which P, Q, R and S are mid-points of sides AB, BC, CD and DA respectively. AC is a diagonal. Show that SR ‚ą• AC and SR = ¬ĹAC.
subject: mathematics
answer: In triangle ACD, S is mid-point of AD and R is mid-point of CD. By the Mid-Point Theorem, SR ‚ą• AC and SR = ¬ĹAC.

question: Find the volume of a sphere whose surface area is 154 cm¬≤. (Use ŌÄ = 22/7.)
subject: mathematics

question: A die is thrown 300 times with the following outcomes: 1‚Üí50, 2‚Üí53, 3‚Üí49, 4‚Üí48, 5‚Üí52, 6‚Üí48. Find the probability of getting (i) 3, (ii) an even number.
subject: mathematics
answer: (i) P(3) = 49/300. (ii) Even numbers: 2, 4, 6 ‚Üí frequencies 53+48+48 = 149. P(even) = 149/300.

question: Find the median of the data: 78, 56, 22, 34, 45, 54, 39, 68, 54, 84.
subject: mathematics

question: Two sides of a triangular plot are 16 m and 12 m and the angle between them is 90¬į. Find the area of the plot using Heron's formula.
subject: mathematics

question: Prove that the angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double the angle subtended by it at any point on the remaining part of the circle.
subject: mathematics
topic: central angle and inscribed angle theorem
answer: Given: Arc PQ of a circle with centre O; A is any point on the major arc. To prove: ‚ą†POQ = 2‚ą†PAQ. Construction: Join AO and extend to B. Proof: OA = OP (radii), so ‚ą†OAP = ‚ą†OPA. By exterior angle, ‚ą†BOP = ‚ą†OAP + ‚ą†OPA = 2‚ą†OAP. Similarly ‚ą†BOQ = 2‚ą†OAQ. Adding: ‚ą†POQ = 2‚ą†PAQ.

question: Represent ‚ąö9.3 on the number line.
subject: mathematics
topic: representing irrational numbers on number line
answer: Mark AB = 9.3 units and BC = 1 unit on the number line. Find mid-point M of AC. Draw a semicircle with centre M and radius MA. Draw a perpendicular BD at B meeting the semicircle at D. BD = ‚ąö9.3. With B as centre and BD as radius, mark the point on the number line.

question: Simplify: (3 + ‚ąö3)(2 + ‚ąö2) / (2 + ‚ąö2).
subject: mathematics

question: If a + b + c = 15 and a¬≤ + b¬≤ + c¬≤ = 83, find the value of a¬≥ + b¬≥ + c¬≥ ‚ąí 3abc.
subject: mathematics
answer: a¬≥+b¬≥+c¬≥‚ąí3abc = (a+b+c)(a¬≤+b¬≤+c¬≤‚ąíab‚ąíbc‚ąíca). First find ab+bc+ca: (a+b+c)¬≤ = a¬≤+b¬≤+c¬≤+2(ab+bc+ca) ‚Üí 225 = 83 + 2(ab+bc+ca) ‚Üí ab+bc+ca = 71. So a¬≤+b¬≤+c¬≤‚ąíab‚ąíbc‚ąíca = 83‚ąí71 = 12. Answer = 15 √ó 12 = 180.

question: Find the value of k if x = 2 is a zero of the polynomial p(x) = 2x¬≥ + kx¬≤ ‚ąí 11x ‚ąí 12.
subject: mathematics

question: Factorise: 2x¬≥ ‚ąí 5x¬≤ ‚ąí 19x + 42.
subject: mathematics
answer: Test x = 2: p(2) = 16 ‚ąí 20 ‚ąí 38 + 42 = 0, so (x ‚ąí 2) is a factor. Divide: 2x¬≥‚ąí5x¬≤‚ąí19x+42 = (x‚ąí2)(2x¬≤‚ąíx‚ąí21) = (x‚ąí2)(2x‚ąí7)(x+3).

question: The taxi fare in a city is as follows: for the first kilometre, the fare is ‚āĻ8, and for subsequent kilometres the fare is ‚āĻ5 per km. Taking the distance covered as x km and total fare as ‚āĻy, write a linear equation for this information and draw its graph.
subject: mathematics
answer: For x ‚Č• 1: y = 8 + 5(x ‚ąí 1) = 5x + 3. So the equation is y = 5x + 3 or 5x ‚ąí y + 3 = 0. Points: (0, 3), (1, 8), (2, 13). Plot and join.

question: In triangle PQR, ‚ą†Q = 80¬į and ‚ą†R = 40¬į. The bisectors of ‚ą†Q and ‚ą†R meet at S. Find ‚ą†QSR.
subject: mathematics
answer: ‚ą†P = 180¬į ‚ąí 80¬į ‚ąí 40¬į = 60¬į. In triangle QSR: ‚ą†SQR = 40¬į, ‚ą†SRQ = 20¬į. So ‚ą†QSR = 180¬į ‚ąí 40¬į ‚ąí 20¬į = 120¬į. (Also: ‚ą†QSR = 90¬į + ‚ą†P/2 = 90¬į + 30¬į = 120¬į.)

question: Prove that the sum of all angles of a quadrilateral is 360¬į.
subject: mathematics
answer: Draw diagonal BD in quadrilateral ABCD, dividing it into triangles ABD and BCD. Sum of angles of each triangle = 180¬į. So sum of all angles of quadrilateral = 180¬į + 180¬į = 360¬į.

question: Equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the centre. Prove it.
subject: mathematics
answer: Given: AB = CD are chords of a circle with centre O. In triangles OAB and OCD: OA = OC (radii), OB = OD (radii), AB = CD (given). By SSS congruence, ‚Ė≥OAB ‚ČÖ ‚Ė≥OCD. Therefore ‚ą†AOB = ‚ą†COD (CPCT).

question: The lateral surface area of a cube is 256 m¬≤. Find the side of the cube and its volume.
subject: mathematics
answer: LSA = 4a¬≤ = 256 ‚Üí a¬≤ = 64 ‚Üí a = 8 m. Volume = a¬≥ = 512 m¬≥.

question: The mode of the data 14, 20, 19, 14, 15, 16, 15, 14, 15, 18, 19, 14, 15, 18, 15 is ___.
subject: mathematics
answer: Frequency count: 14‚Üí4, 15‚Üí5, 16‚Üí1, 18‚Üí2, 19‚Üí2, 20‚Üí1. Mode = 15 (highest frequency).

question: A bag contains 4 white, 6 red and 5 black balls. A ball is drawn at random. What is the probability that the ball is (i) white, (ii) not red?
subject: mathematics

question: A rhombus-shaped field has green grass for 18 cows to graze. The side of the rhombus is 30 m and its longer diagonal is 48 m. How much area of grass field will each cow be getting?
subject: mathematics
topic: area of rhombus using Heron's formula
answer: Half of longer diagonal = 24 m. Semi-perimeter of each triangle: s = (30 + 30 + 48)/2 = 54 m. Area of each triangle = ‚ąö[54 √ó 24 √ó 24 √ó 6] = ‚ąö186624 = 432 m¬≤. Total area = 864 m¬≤. Each cow gets 864/18 = 48 m¬≤.

question: If lines l ‚ą• m, what is the value of x in the figure where one angle on line l is (3x + 20)¬į and the corresponding angle on line m is (5x ‚ąí 40)¬į?
subject: mathematics
answer: Since l ‚ą• m, corresponding angles are equal: 3x + 20 = 5x ‚ąí 40 ‚Üí 60 = 2x ‚Üí x = 30.

question: In a parallelogram ABCD, the diagonal AC and BD bisect each other at O. If AC = 20 cm and BD = 24 cm, find AB.
subject: mathematics
answer: Diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other: OA = AC/2 = 10 cm, OB = BD/2 = 12 cm. However, the side AB of a general parallelogram cannot be determined from diagonal lengths alone ‚ÄĒ the angle between the diagonals is not known. If additional information (e.g., that ABCD is a rectangle) were given, AB could be found. As stated, the problem is incomplete. Note: if the diagonals were perpendicular (rhombus), AB = ‚ąö(10¬≤ + 12¬≤) = ‚ąö244 = 2‚ąö61 cm.

question: Construct a triangle XYZ where ‚ą†Y = 30¬į, ‚ą†Z = 90¬į and XY + YZ + XZ = 11 cm.
subject: mathematics
answer: Steps: (1) Draw a line segment PQ = 11 cm. (2) At P, draw ray at ‚ą†Y/2 = 15¬į. (3) At Q, draw ray at ‚ą†Z/2 = 45¬į. (4) Let these rays meet at X. (5) Draw perpendicular bisectors of PX and QX to meet PQ at Y and Z. (6) Join XY and XZ. Triangle XYZ is the required triangle.

question: Evaluate: (‚ąö5 + ‚ąö2)¬≤ + (‚ąö5 ‚ąí ‚ąö2)¬≤.
subject: mathematics

question: If 2^(x+1) = 4^(x‚ąí3), find the value of x.
subject: mathematics

question: Factorise: 12x¬≤ ‚ąí 7x + 1.
subject: mathematics
answer: Product = 12 √ó 1 = 12; factors of 12 that subtract to ‚ąí7: ‚ąí3 and ‚ąí4. 12x¬≤ ‚ąí 3x ‚ąí 4x + 1 = 3x(4x ‚ąí 1) ‚ąí 1(4x ‚ąí 1) = (3x ‚ąí 1)(4x ‚ąí 1).

question: If (x ‚ąí 1/x) = 3, find the value of x¬≥ ‚ąí 1/x¬≥.
subject: mathematics

question: Give the geometric representation of the equation 3x + 7 = 0 on (i) the number line (ii) the Cartesian plane.
subject: mathematics
answer: x = ‚ąí7/3. (i) On number line: mark the point ‚ąí7/3 ‚Čą ‚ąí2.33. (ii) On Cartesian plane: it is a vertical line parallel to the y-axis passing through (‚ąí7/3, 0).

question: In triangle ABC, AB = AC and ‚ą†A = 40¬į. Bisectors of ‚ą†B and ‚ą†C meet at O. Find ‚ą†BOC.
subject: mathematics

question: In a triangle, the side BC is produced to D. If ‚ą†ACD = 108¬į and ‚ą†B = (1/2)‚ą†A, find ‚ą†A and ‚ą†B.
subject: mathematics
answer: Exterior angle ‚ą†ACD = ‚ą†A + ‚ą†B. So 108¬į = ‚ą†A + (1/2)‚ą†A = (3/2)‚ą†A ‚Üí ‚ą†A = 72¬į, ‚ą†B = 36¬į.

question: If two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, prove that the bisectors of the interior angles form a rectangle.
subject: mathematics
answer: Let AB ‚ą• CD, transversal EF intersecting at P and Q. Interior angles at P and Q: ‚ą†BPQ + ‚ą†PQD = 180¬į (co-interior angles). Angle bisectors make (1/2)(‚ą†BPQ) + (1/2)(‚ą†PQD) = 90¬į. In the quadrilateral formed by the four bisectors, each angle = 90¬į, so it is a rectangle.

question: In parallelogram ABCD, the bisector of ‚ą†A also bisects BC at X. Prove that AD = 2AB.
subject: mathematics
answer: AX bisects ‚ą†A and bisects BC (BX = XC). Since AB ‚ą• DC, ‚ą†BAX = ‚ą†AXC (alternate angles). Also ‚ą†BAX = ‚ą†DAX (bisector). So ‚ą†AXC = ‚ą†DAX ‚Üí AD ‚ą• ... Using triangle ABX: ‚ą†ABX = ‚ą†BAX (isosceles ‚Üí AB = BX). So BX = AB. Since BX = BC/2, BC = 2AB. AD = BC (parallelogram), so AD = 2AB.

question: A field is in the shape of a trapezium. Its parallel sides are 25 m and 10 m. The non-parallel sides are 14 m and 13 m. Find the area of the field.
subject: mathematics
topic: area of trapezium using Heron's formula
answer: Draw CE ‚ą• DA where E is on AB. EB = AB ‚ąí AE = 25 ‚ąí 10 = 15 m. In triangle BCE: sides 14, 13, 15. s = 21. Area = ‚ąö[21√ó7√ó8√ó6] = ‚ąö7056 = 84 m¬≤. Height h = 2√ó84/15 = 11.2 m. Area of trapezium = ¬Ĺ(25 + 10) √ó 11.2 = 196 m¬≤.

question: In the given figure, AB is a chord of circle with centre O and AOC is a diameter such that ‚ą†ACB = 50¬į. If AT is the tangent to the circle at point A, find ‚ą†BAT and ‚ą†ATB.
subject: mathematics
topic: angle in a semicircle and tangent
answer: [NOTE: This question uses the Alternate Segment Theorem, which is a Class 10 topic (CBSE). It is beyond the Class 9 syllabus.] ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į (angle in semicircle, since AOC is a diameter). In triangle ABC: ‚ą†BAC = 180¬į ‚ąí 90¬į ‚ąí 50¬į = 40¬į. The tangent AT at A is perpendicular to the radius OA, so ‚ą†OAT = 90¬į. ‚ą†OAB = ‚ą†OBA (OA = OB, radii) ‚ÄĒ but OA is the radius. By the Alternate Segment Theorem (Class 10): ‚ą†BAT = ‚ą†ACB = 50¬į. In triangle ABT: ‚ą†TAB = 50¬į, ‚ą†ABT = 180¬į ‚ąí ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į, so ‚ą†ATB = 180¬į ‚ąí 50¬į ‚ąí 90¬į = 40¬į.

question: The length, breadth and height of a room are 5 m, 4 m and 3 m respectively. Find the cost of white washing all four walls and the ceiling at the rate of ‚āĻ7.50 per m¬≤.
subject: mathematics
answer: Area of 4 walls = 2(l + b) √ó h = 2(5 + 4) √ó 3 = 54 m¬≤. Ceiling area = l √ó b = 20 m¬≤. Total = 74 m¬≤. Cost = 74 √ó 7.50 = ‚āĻ555.

question: The mean of 100 observations is 50. If one of the observations which was 50 is replaced by 150, what will be the resulting mean?
subject: mathematics

question: There are 50 numbers. Each number is subtracted from 53 and the mean of the numbers so obtained is found to be ‚ąí3.5. Find the mean of the given 50 numbers.
subject: mathematics
answer: Mean of (53 ‚ąí x_i) = ‚ąí3.5 ‚Üí 53 ‚ąí mean(x_i) = ‚ąí3.5 ‚Üí mean(x_i) = 56.5.

question: A coin is tossed 1000 times with the following frequencies: Head = 455, Tail = 545. Compute the probability for each event.
subject: mathematics

question: Find the volume of a right circular cone with (i) radius 6 cm, height 7 cm  (ii) radius 3.5 cm, height 12 cm. (Use ŌÄ = 22/7.)
subject: mathematics

question: ABCD is a rectangle with ‚ą†ABD = 40¬į. Determine ‚ą†DBC.
subject: mathematics
answer: In rectangle ABCD, ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į. So ‚ą†DBC = ‚ą†ABC ‚ąí ‚ą†ABD = 90¬į ‚ąí 40¬į = 50¬į.

question: In the figure, O is the centre of the circle. If ‚ą†OAB = 40¬į and ‚ą†OCB = 30¬į, find ‚ą†AOC.
subject: mathematics
topic: angles subtended at centre and circumference
answer: OA = OB = OC (radii). ‚ą†OAB = ‚ą†OBA = 40¬į ‚Üí ‚ą†AOB = 100¬į. ‚ą†OCB = ‚ą†OBC = 30¬į ‚Üí ‚ą†BOC = 120¬į. ‚ą†AOC = 360¬į ‚ąí ‚ą†AOB ‚ąí ‚ą†BOC = 360¬į ‚ąí 100¬į ‚ąí 120¬į = 140¬į.
